network_dim: LoRA rank (dimension of low-rank matrices). Higher = more expressive but more VRAM. Typical: 8–64.
network_alpha: LoRA scaling factor. Effective scale = alpha / dim. When alpha == dim, scale is 1.0. Lower alpha = more conservative updates.
network_module: Python module path for the LoRA network implementation.
use_timestep_mask: Enable T-LoRA: effective rank varies with denoising timestep via power-law schedule. Full rank at high noise, reduced at low noise.
use_ortho: Enable OrthoLoRA: SVD-based orthogonal parameterization of the update matrix (linear layers only). Regularizes toward structured updates; saved as plain LoRA via thin SVD at checkpoint time.
use_moe_style: MoE expert layout: 'shared_A' (HydraLoRA — one shared lora_down + N per-expert lora_up heads), 'independent_A' (FeRA — N fully-independent down/up pairs), or false (no MoE). Produces a *_moe.safetensors sibling for router-live inference; requires cache_llm_adapter_outputs=true.
route_per_layer: If true, each layer owns its own router (per-layer routing). If false, a single network-level GlobalRouter broadcasts gate weights to every routed module.
router_source: Routing signal: 'sigma' (sinusoidal embedding of the denoising timestep), 'fei' (mean-pooled rank features from preceding LoRA modules), or 'pooled_text' (pooled T5 caption embedding).
num_experts: HydraLoRA expert count. More experts = more capacity but more VRAM and slower training. Typical: 2–8.
balance_loss_weight: HydraLoRA load-balancing loss weight. Discourages router collapse onto a single expert. Typical: 0.01.
balance_loss_warmup_ratio: Fraction of training steps to hold the balance loss at 0 before activating it. Lets the router specialize first, then switches the penalty on to stop further collapse of a diverged router. 0.0 disables the warmup. Typical: 0.3–0.5.
add_reft: Enable ReFT: block-level residual-stream intervention (Wu et al. 2024). Adds R^T·(ΔW·h + b)·scale to each selected DiT block's output. Composes with any LoRA variant.
reft_dim: ReFT intervention rank — dimension of R and ΔW in each ReFTModule. Typical: 32–64.
reft_alpha: ReFT scaling factor (effective scale = alpha / dim). Typical: same as reft_dim.
reft_layers: Which DiT blocks receive ReFT modules. 'all', 'last_8', 'first_4', 'stride_2', or comma-separated indices like '3,7,11,15'.
sigma_feature_dim: Sinusoidal σ feature dimension fed into the σ-router bias MLP. Typical: 128.
router_targets: Regex over layer names — only matching Linears participate in routed adaptation (Hydra MoE leaves + σ / FEI feature concatenation share the same scope). Typical: '.*(mlp\.layer[12])$' to confine MoE to the FFN sublayers.
per_bucket_balance_weight: Extra per-σ-bucket load-balance penalty, scaled by balance_loss_weight. Encourages routing diversity within each timestep bucket. Typical: 0.3.
num_sigma_buckets: Number of timestep buckets used for per-bucket balance accounting. Typical: 3 (low / mid / high noise).
specialize_experts_by_sigma_buckets: Hard-partition the expert pool into σ-bands: each timestep bucket only routes to its assigned experts. Forces specialization on top of the soft σ-router bias. Pairs with sigma_bucket_boundaries.
sigma_bucket_boundaries: Custom σ-bucket edges, length = num_sigma_buckets + 1, monotone 0.0 → 1.0. Defaults to uniform linspace(0, 1, N+1) when omitted. Example: [0.0, 0.5, 0.8, 1.0].
network_args: Extra kwargs passed to the network module. For postfix: list of 'key=value' strings (e.g., 'mode=cond', 'splice_position=end_of_sequence', 'cond_hidden_dim=256'). Pick a Variant to auto-fill.
min_rank: Minimum active rank when T-LoRA timestep masking is enabled. At the lowest-noise timesteps, rank drops to this value.
alpha_rank_scale: Scale alpha proportionally when T-LoRA reduces rank, keeping effective learning rate stable across timesteps.
network_train_unet_only: Train only the DiT (U-Net). Text encoder weights are frozen. Recommended for most LoRA training.
network_weights: Path to a pre-trained adapter checkpoint to warm-start from. Leave empty for plain LoRA training.
dim_from_weights: Read network_dim from the warm-start checkpoint instead of the form value. Set together with network_weights so rank matches the warm-start LoRA.
learning_rate: Base learning rate for the optimizer. Typical: 1e-5 to 1e-4.
max_train_epochs: Total training epochs. One epoch = one full pass through the dataset.
save_every_n_epochs: Save a checkpoint every N epochs. Set equal to max_train_epochs to save only the final model.
checkpointing_epochs: Save resumable training state every N epochs. State files are large; use a larger interval than save_every_n_epochs.
gradient_accumulation_steps: Accumulate gradients over N steps before updating. Effective batch size = batch_size × accumulation_steps.
use_shuffled_caption_variants: Consume preprocessed caption-shuffle variants from the text-encoder cache. When the cache holds multiple variants, a random one is drawn per sample. Falls back silently to single-variant if no variants were preprocessed.
caption_dropout_rate: Probability per sample of dropping the caption (replaced with empty text embedding). Pushes the LoRA toward an unconditional bias — useful for style training where you want the look to apply regardless of prompt. Typical: 0.0–0.05 for character/concept LoRAs, 0.1–0.25 for style LoRAs (그림체 학습). Too high can blur prompt-driven diversity (pose/composition).
optimizer_type: Optimizer algorithm. AdamW8bit: memory-efficient 8-bit Adam. Others: AdamW, Lion, Prodigy, etc.
lr_scheduler: Learning rate schedule. constant: fixed LR. Others: cosine, cosine_with_restarts, polynomial.
timestep_sampling: How denoising timesteps are sampled during training. sigmoid: biased toward middle timesteps (recommended for flow matching).
discrete_flow_shift: Flow-matching shift parameter controlling the noise schedule distribution. Default: 1.0.
attn_mode: Attention backend. flash4: FlashAttention-4 (Linux, fastest). flash: FlashAttention-2. flex: PyTorch flex attention (cross-platform).
gradient_checkpointing: Recompute activations during backward pass instead of storing them. Trades compute for VRAM. Essential for low-VRAM setups.
unsloth_offload_checkpointing: Offload gradient checkpoints to CPU RAM. Further VRAM reduction at cost of speed. Requires gradient_checkpointing=true.
blocks_to_swap: Number of DiT blocks to swap between GPU and CPU. 0: all on GPU. Higher values = more CPU offloading for low VRAM.
torch_compile: Enable torch.compile for the forward pass. Faster training after initial compilation. Compiles each DiT block at native shapes — one graph per token-count bucket family, no static padding.
cache_llm_adapter_outputs: Cache the LLM adapter layer outputs to disk. Avoids recomputing text encoder projections each epoch.
masked_loss: Apply loss only to non-masked regions (e.g., exclude text bubbles). Requires mask files in post_image_dataset/masks/ (or legacy masks/merged/) — run `make mask` to generate them.
mixed_precision: Mixed precision mode. bf16: recommended for modern GPUs. fp16: for older GPUs without bf16 support.
vae_chunk_size: VAE decoding chunk size. Larger = faster but more VRAM. 64 is a good balance.
vae_disable_cache: Disable VAE's internal KV cache. Reduces VRAM during VAE encoding/decoding.
cache_latents: Cache VAE-encoded latents in memory. Avoids re-encoding images every epoch.
cache_latents_to_disk: Save cached latents to disk instead of RAM. Frees system memory at cost of disk I/O.
cache_text_encoder_outputs: Cache text encoder outputs. Essential for lazy loading: encode → cache → free encoder → load DiT.
cache_text_encoder_outputs_to_disk: Save cached text encoder outputs to disk. Required for the lazy loading sequence to free VRAM before loading DiT.
skip_cache_check: Skip validation of cached files on startup. Faster startup when caches are known to be valid.
use_cmmd: Use CMMD (PE-Core MMD²) as the validation signal. Off by default in the GUI — CMMD adds the PE encoder + a sampling pass per held-out item, which costs extra VRAM and time. Off → falls back to the cheaper per-σ FM-MSE val pass (uninformative on Anima but free).
use_valid: Hold out a small validation slice from the training set (16 images by default — pinned via base.toml's validation_split_num). When off, the whole pool is used for training and no validation pass runs — skips the FM-MSE / CMMD eval and lets very small datasets train without losing samples. Writes/strips a {validation_split_num = 0, validation_split = 0.0} override on the variant's [[datasets]] block; base.toml is not touched.
validation_split_num: How many images to hold out for validation when 'use_valid' is on. Base.toml ships 16 — large enough for stable paired CMMD, small enough to barely dent the train pool. Bigger values give a quieter CMMD curve at the cost of fewer training samples and a slower val pass. Ignored when 'use_valid' is off (the zero-override wins). Writes/strips a {validation_split_num = N} override on the variant's [[datasets]] block; matching base.toml's value strips the override so base wins.
pretrained_model_name_or_path: Path to the base DiT model weights (.safetensors).
qwen3: Path to the Qwen3 text encoder weights for text-to-image conditioning.
vae: Path to the VAE model for image encoding/decoding.
output_dir: Directory for saving trained LoRA checkpoints.
output_name: Base filename for saved checkpoints (epoch number is appended automatically).
source_image_dir: Where raw images and .txt captions live. The Preprocess button feeds this to resize_images.py (writes resized PNGs) and cache_text_embeddings.py (caches captions). Override per preset/method if you keep multiple datasets side by side.
resized_image_dir: Where preprocess writes VAE-aligned PNGs. Also resolved into the dataset subset's image_dir at training time (via {resized_image_dir} template in base.toml), so editing this propagates to both preprocess and training.
lora_cache_dir: Where preprocess writes VAE latent (.npz) and text-encoder (_anima_te.safetensors) caches. Also resolved into the dataset subset's cache_dir at training time.
path_pattern: fnmatch glob applied to each image's path relative to its subset's image_dir. Lets one variant TOML train on a slice of the full dataset without re-running preprocessing. `|` separates alternatives — OR-combine multiple patterns. Examples: `*` keeps everything; `char_a/*` keeps only files under the char_a/ subfolder; `char_a/*|char_b/*` keeps either folder; `*portrait*` keeps anything whose path contains 'portrait'. Validation enumeration and the image-count threshold honour the filtered pool.
drop_lowres_images: When true, the preprocess auto-chain skips source images below the `min_pixels` threshold so they never enter the resize / VAE / TE caches. Applied to both preprocess/resize_images.py and preprocess/cache_text_embeddings.py via the same `--min_pixels` argument. Uncheck to keep every image regardless of size.
min_pixels: Pixel-count threshold used by `drop_lowres_images` (default 500_000 = 0.5MP). Forwarded verbatim to preprocess/resize_images.py and cache_text_embeddings.py as `--min_pixels`. Ignored when `drop_lowres_images = false`. Set to 0 (with the flag on) to disable the filter without flipping the checkbox.
